title: Dense, sparse, and late-interaction embeddings
description: How single-vector dense, learned-sparse, and multi-vector late-interaction representations differ in recall behavior.
body: Use when choosing a representation for retrieval. Dense single-vector embeddings capture semantics compactly but blur exact terms; learned-sparse representations (e.g. SPLADE) keep term-level signal and suit lexical-heavy queries; late-interaction models (e.g. ColBERT) embed per token and match finely at higher cost. Do not assume dense is always best — exact-match-heavy domains favor sparse or hybrid. Actions: match representation to query type and benchmark recall. Validation signals include a recall comparison across representations and a chosen approach justified by the eval. Risk boundary: late-interaction storage and latency are heavy; if its cost exceeds budget, escalate the tradeoff rather than silently degrading.
